next_best_action: Investigate subspace relay handshake compatibility with the latest comm stack firmware update. Check for known signal issues on Antenna Grid B, Deck 7, Sector 4-Alpha. Verify SubspaceLink v3.2 is the latest supported version.
remediation_steps: Verify SubspaceLink v3.2 is the latest supported version for the comm stack
Check for known signal interference issues on Antenna Grid B, Deck 7, Sector 4-Alpha
Test relay handshake behavior after the latest firmware update on a reference antenna
If issue persists, escalate to communications engineering for firmware patch review

next_best_action: Investigate whether NavPlotter crash and starchart sync issues are related to the console swap. Verify that crew profile and application data were properly transferred to the new console.
remediation_steps: Verify console provisioning and data migration completed successfully
Clear NavPlotter cache and reinstall if crash persists
Check starchart profile migration status and force waypoint resync
Confirm software versions match the supported standard console image
If issues persist after cache clear and resync, re-provision applications on the new console

next_best_action: Dispatch immediate on-site crew to Airlock 7 with a portable backup docking alignment module. Time-critical: Velorian diplomatic shuttle arriving in 25 minutes for a Class-1 alliance treaty ceremony.
remediation_steps: Dispatch engineering team to Airlock 7 immediately
Bring portable backup docking alignment module from engineering supply bay
Check alignment system power source, data conduit connections, and sensor calibration
If primary system cannot be restored, deploy portable unit before shuttle arrival
After docking is complete, schedule full diagnostic of the Airlock 7 alignment system

next_best_action: Contact crew member to gather specifics: which subsystems are inaccessible, what error readouts appear, what console they are using, and when the issue started. Cannot triage effectively without more detail.
remediation_steps: Contact crew member to gather specific details about the issue
Determine which station subsystems and applications are affected
Check if the issue is isolated to this crew member or affecting others in Communications
Once root cause is identified, route to the appropriate specialist team

next_best_action: Close — this is an automated EVA-absence reply to a maintenance notification, not a support request. No action required.
remediation_steps: No action required — auto-reply detected, close signal

next_best_action: Initiate security incident investigation. Review authentication logs for unauthorized access, check personal data vault audit trail for document access from the suspicious sector, and verify that the retinal hash reset and biometric lock are in effect.
remediation_steps: Review authentication logs for the crew member's profile over the past 48 hours
Check personal data vault audit trail for any unauthorized file access or downloads
Verify retinal hash reset was successful and biometric lock is active
If unauthorized access is confirmed, escalate to incident response and notify the station data protection officer
Brief crew member on interim security measures and monitor profile for further anomalies

next_best_action: Check service principal credentials and clearance assignments on the Deep Storage Array Gen2 data banks in rg-telemetry-prod. Restore the Storage Data Contributor clearance if it was removed during the data bank reorganization.
remediation_steps: Verify service principal token expiration for the telemetry pipeline identity
Check clearance assignments on the Deep Storage Array Gen2 data banks in rg-telemetry-prod
Restore Storage Data Contributor clearance if missing
Re-run the pipeline 'prod-sensor-ingest' and verify successful execution
Investigate why the clearance was removed during the data bank reorganization to prevent recurrence

next_best_action: Investigate crew telemetry database connection pool exhaustion immediately. This is a station-wide production incident — 40% of queries are failing regardless of the submitter's priority classification.
remediation_steps: Investigate connection pool configuration on the primary telemetry data node
Identify and terminate long-running queries or leaked connections
Increase connection pool size or restart the primary data node as immediate mitigation
Verify backup node health and confirm it is handling redirected traffic
Post-incident: conduct root cause analysis on connection pool exhaustion

next_best_action: Escalate airlock access issue for Module 7 — third report without resolution. Verify badge permissions in the updated airlock access system post-migration and cross-reference with SIG-2834 if accessible.
remediation_steps: Check crew member's badge permissions in the updated airlock access system
Verify Module 7 access rights were properly migrated from the old system
If permissions appear correct, test the physical badge and airlock reader
Escalate to station facilities management if badge and permissions are both confirmed working
Follow up with crew member to confirm resolution

next_best_action: Initiate new crew onboarding workflow for Alex Rivera (arrival date March 31). Coordinate console, software, access, and comm group provisioning across teams. Target readiness by March 28.
remediation_steps: Order NebulaStation Pro X4 32GB quantum RAM from hardware procurement
Prepare standard software image with AstroCalc, StellarSim, and JupyterNova environment
Request Deep Scan Lab access (rg-scans-prod) from science operations
Request starchart archive read access from data engineering
Request Module 3 airlock badge from crew identity operations
Add Alex Rivera to astro-team comm channel and briefing group
Schedule console handoff and walkthrough for March 28

next_best_action: Investigate retinal scanner authentication failure in the BioAuth panel. Check crew identity registration status and recent biometric policy changes. Crew member can continue with manual keycode fallback in the meantime.
remediation_steps: Verify biometric registration status for this crew member in the identity system
Check for recent biometric policy or scanner firmware changes that may affect retinal scans
Have crew member verify BioAuth panel firmware is updated to the latest version
If issue persists, remove and re-register the biometric profile in the crew identity system
Confirm retinal authentication works after re-registration

next_best_action: Investigate viewport display flickering as primary issue — likely hardware or plasma conduit fault. Route the StellarNav license question separately to Mission Software Operations.
remediation_steps: Test with a replacement plasma conduit cable
Test viewport display with a different console source to isolate whether it's the display or the sensor array
If flickering persists with new conduit and source, schedule viewport display replacement
Create a separate signal for the StellarNav license expiry and route to Mission Software Operations

next_best_action: Initiate data breach investigation immediately. This is a potential regulatory incident involving crew PII exposure of 1,200 personnel under Interstellar Crew Privacy Regulations (ICPR). Determine scope of external access, identify who created the sharing link, and assess notification obligations.
remediation_steps: Confirm the external sharing link has been fully revoked and verify no other vaults have similar permissions
Pull data vault audit logs to identify who created the public sharing link and when
Analyze audit logs for any external access to the vault between stardate 2426-02-15 and today
Engage Xenolinguistics and Mission Compliance to assess ICPR notification obligations
Document findings for the incident report and regulatory record
Conduct a broader data vault permissions audit across all vaults containing crew PII

next_best_action: Close — this is a thank-you message, not a support request. No action required.
remediation_steps: No action required — positive feedback, close signal

next_best_action: Investigate 200ms→50ms latency regression on the port maneuvering array thruster response before the orbital correction window. Check data path to the thruster gimbal actuators, recent flight computer or routing changes, and whether the latency is on the station's side or the actuator side.
remediation_steps: Check response latency metrics on the path between CDSS flight computer and the port thruster gimbal actuators
Review recent flight computer, routing, or shield barrier changes that may affect the thruster control path
Compare current latency baseline against historical data to confirm the regression
If latency is on the station's side, escalate to flight systems engineering for immediate remediation before orbital correction window
If actuator-side, contact thruster maintenance and notify flight deck crew of potential impact

next_best_action: Investigate SSO token invalidation after credential rotation. Likely the NavPlotter SSO session needs to be reissued after the station credential change. Time-sensitive due to end-of-cycle orbital recalibration deadline.
remediation_steps: Verify the crew member's identity session and authentication token are refreshed after the credential rotation
Check NavPlotter SSO configuration — confirm whether NavPlotter uses token-based or certificate-based SSO
Reissue the NavPlotter SSO session token or have the crew member log in with explicit credentials as a workaround
If SSO token regeneration doesn't resolve it, check whether the credential rotation policy affected the NavPlotter security configuration
Confirm NavPlotter access is restored and the crew member can complete end-of-cycle orbital recalibration

next_best_action: Contact reporter on Deck 5 (Robert Chen, robert.chen2@contoso.com, Life Support) to gather actual symptom details. Signal as submitted has insufficient detail to triage. Emergency beacon transcription is too garbled to act on.
remediation_steps: Contact Robert Chen (Deck 5 near mess hall, Life Support department) to clarify the issue in person or via holodeck comm
Determine which application or subsystem is failing
Gather specific anomaly readouts and reproduction steps
Once the actual issue is identified, re-triage and route to the correct specialist team

next_best_action: Dispatch support to the Deck 8 command theater immediately. Admiral-level VIP signal — treat as one priority level higher. Investigate station security policy blocking tactical readout projection for this crew member. Active command briefing with Joint Fleet Admiralty.
remediation_steps: Dispatch technician to the Deck 8 command theater immediately
Check security policies and projection permissions applied to Admiral Okoro's profile
Verify whether the 'Projection blocked by station security policy' error is caused by a data protection policy, HoloSync admin policy, or security restriction
As immediate workaround: have someone else project the strategic overlay on his behalf, or use the HoloSync device with a different profile
Once the briefing is unblocked, investigate and resolve the underlying policy conflict

next_best_action: Initiate crew departure workflow for Jessica Palmer (last day March 21). Coordinate across Crew Identity, Spacecraft Systems Engineering, and Telemetry & Data Core for profile disable, console wipe, file transfer, and message archival. Note: sensitive mission compliance files require special handling.

Archive message logs per 7-cycle retention policy

next_best_action: Investigate fabrication unit queue issue on Deck 3. Multiple crew affected and jobs are queuing but not materializing, which suggests a network or control system issue rather than a single-console problem.
remediation_steps: Identify the fabrication unit model and check whether it is network-attached or connected via station control server
Check fabrication control server spooler service status and restart if necessary
Verify data link connectivity between the fabrication unit and the control server or direct network path
If network-connected, check the fabricator's network address and interface status
Clear stuck fabrication queue and test with a fresh job

next_best_action: Redirect to Station Operations — holodeck reservation and catering are not mission operations support functions. Provide brief guidance on how to reserve the holodeck via StationOS scheduler (add facility resource) and direct catering questions to Station Operations.
remediation_steps: Reply with instructions: in StationOS scheduler, create a new event and use 'Add a facility' to search for available holodecks
Note that catering requests should go to Station Operations, not mission ops
If the crew member cannot see facility resources in the scheduler, verify their profile has the facility finder permission and route to Mission Software Operations if needed

next_best_action: Renew or replace the encryption certificate on comms-relay.contoso.com before it expires on March 20. This is a production external communications relay — expiry will break all allied fleet subspace transmissions. Treat as P1 given 3-day window and the fact that the original provisioner has transferred off-station.
remediation_steps: Identify the current certificate authority and certificate type (public CA, internal PKI, or automated provisioning)
Determine whether auto-renewal was configured or if the cert was manually provisioned
If auto-renewal exists, trigger a manual renewal cycle and verify the new cert is issued
If manual, generate a certificate signing request, obtain a new certificate from the CA, and install it on the comms relay
Verify the new certificate is correctly installed and all subspace transmissions work without encryption errors
Configure auto-renewal and monitoring alerts to prevent recurrence

next_best_action: Investigate console performance degradation. Symptoms (slow performance, cooling fans running, pop-ups about 'updating system firmware') are more consistent with a stuck firmware update or background compliance policy sync than a security compromise, especially since ShieldScan found nothing. Check update history and system resource usage.
remediation_steps: Check firmware update history — verify whether recent updates completed or are stuck retrying
Review system monitor for high CPU/memory processes (likely firmware update or compliance policy sync)
If updates are stuck, run the station firmware troubleshooter or manually reset the update components
Verify ShieldScan definitions are current and run a quick scan to confirm no threats
If the crew member opened a suspicious data packet, forward it to threats@contoso.com and notify Threat Response Command as a precaution

next_best_action: Confirm this is a phishing/scam transmission and advise the crew member NOT to open any links or transmit any information. Forward the original transmission to threats@contoso.com for analysis. No breach has occurred if the crew member hasn't interacted with it.
remediation_steps: Reply to the crew member immediately: this is a scam transmission — do NOT open any links or provide any personal information
Have the crew member forward the original transmission as an attachment to threats@contoso.com
Check whether the transmission bypassed CDSS comm filtering and report to Threat Response Command if so
If the crew member already opened the link, escalate to a full security incident and initiate credential reset

next_best_action: Investigate compute module prod-analytics-01 stuck at 'Applying station configuration' after scheduled firmware patching reboot. Command staff analytics dashboard dependent on this module — 2-hour window before the 09:00 briefing.
remediation_steps: Check boot diagnostics serial console output for the stuck state on the compute module
Attempt to connect via the station serial console to bypass the stuck configuration screen
If serial console access is possible, check if a startup script or configuration policy is hanging
If the module is unrecoverable, redeploy from the latest snapshot or restore from backup
Verify the analytics dashboard is accessible once the module is restored
Post-incident: review the firmware patching procedure to prevent boot failures after scheduled updates
